text:
0:52
That statement at the corner is exactly what I always think when I see this commercial.
Disco and its aesthetics didn't become popular until the mid 1970s from around 1974-1976. The predominant music scene and aesthetic associated with the year 1970 was actually a fusion of Psychedelia & early Metal. Another thing that irks me with that depiction of 1970 is that they act like having no hair was a trend in the 1970s. If anything, it was the opposite. In fact, the 1970s is probably most well known for the over abundance of hair. I don't know what kind of shit they were smoking to depict the year 1970 as a place where hairless people were dancing to disco. If you want a good depiction of what the year 1970 was like culturally, look into the David Bowie album "The Man Who Sold The World"
